metadata.note: Preselected hardwired image URLs. No runtime Commons search in the app; expanded plant coverage with verified direct file-page references and stricter whole-plant-first selection.

wholePlantReview.notes: Reviewed existing species image sets and promoted/replaced first images so the lead image is a clearer whole-plant/habit image where available on Wikimedia Commons.

batch_notes.note: Added or expanded 5 Commons-backed species image sets with a habit/whole-plant lead image: bearberries, groundnut, bee-balm, nannyberry, staghorn-sumac.

Added 8 Commons-backed species image sets under the stricter standard: sweet-flag, willow, juniper, chickweed, burdock, daylily, basswood, birch. Each set uses a whole-plant/habit lead image plus two support images.

Added 8 Commons-backed species image sets under the stricter standard: black-currants, chokeberry-aronia, elderberries, gooseberries, hawthorn-berries, juneberries-serviceberries, red-currants, wild-strawberries. Each set uses a whole-plant/habit lead image plus two support images.

Added 8 Commons-backed species image sets under the stricter standard: black-raspberries, eastern-hemlock, hop, purslane, sassafras, wild-carrot, lamb-s-quarters, clover. Each set uses a whole-plant/habit lead image plus two support images.
Added 8 Commons-backed species image sets: angelica, barberries, fiddlehead-ferns, goldenrod, highbush-cranberries-viburnum, honeysuckle, huckleberries, jerusalem-artichoke.
Added 8 Commons-backed species image sets: mulberries, oak, pine, red-sorrel, riverbank-grape, sweet-cicely, violet, wild-plums.
Added 8 Commons-backed image sets / compatibility records: lowbush-cranberries, wild-mustard-greens, wood-sorrel-yellow-woodsorrel, wood-sorrel, elder, hawthorn, pine-needles-tea, pine-resin-gum.

strict_whole_plant_batch_f.policy: Each added species uses a clear whole-plant or habit lead image and two non-redundant supporting images.

strict_whole_plant_batch_g.policy: Each added species uses a clear whole-plant or habit lead image and two non-redundant supporting images.

strict_whole_plant_batch_h.policy: Each added species uses a clear whole-plant or habit lead image and two non-redundant supporting images.

three_batch_run_2026_04_20.policy: Whole-plant/habit lead image where clean Commons coverage existed; some weaker or compatibility records use illustrations or reused species sets to cover remaining app records without creating extra files.
